commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=b8c6ca738bf6a5d7d333c164bb7d937076cd3eee)
commands.review_summary:
Adds a five-model static OpenCode Zen catalog, live model discovery, dynamic resolution, shared credential lookup, base-URL normalization, manifest metadata, and focused catalog tests.

PR surface: Source +435, Tests +239. Total +674 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main lacks the removed shipped Zen catalog, and history identifies the family-aware routing contract that the proposed implementation regresses. This read-only review did not execute a credentialed model completion.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The routing defects are concrete, but fallback scope and shared-auth upgrade behavior still require explicit provider-owner judgment before an automated repair or merge.

target.title: fix(opencode): restore Zen model catalog
target.body:
## Summary

- Restore a plugin-owned OpenCode Zen model catalog for the bundled `opencode` provider.
- Wire Zen catalog runtime hooks: static curated seed, live `/zen/v1/models` discovery, dynamic model resolution, model-catalog augmentation, and base URL normalization.
- Add focused coverage for static Claude seed rows, live-discovered-only rows, shared OpenCode auth handling, runtime discovery metadata, and stale Zen base URL canonicalization.

Fixes #92479

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** `opencode` no longer ships without catalog or dynamic-resolution hooks. A configured Zen model such as `opencode/claude-opus-4-8` can resolve from the bundled curated seed, and live discovery can synthesize additional Zen rows from `https://opencode.ai/zen/v1/models` while falling back to static rows when discovery is unavailable.

**Real environment tested:** Linux, Node 22.22.0, pnpm 11.2.2, local OpenClaw checkout at this PR head. Local proof used a dummy OpenCode env key for catalog fallback visibility and mocked live discovery for live-only row synthesis; no real OpenCode credentials or live model requests were used.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
git diff --check
corepack pnpm@11.2.2 --dir . test extensions/opencode/index.test.ts
corepack pnpm@11.2.2 --dir . exec oxlint extensions/opencode/index.ts extensions/opencode/provider-catalog.ts extensions/opencode/index.test.ts
OPENCODE_API_KEY=dummy corepack pnpm@11.2.2 --dir . openclaw models list --provider opencode
corepack pnpm@11.2.2 --dir . plugins:inventory:check
```

**Evidence after fix:** Copied terminal output from a real OpenClaw CLI catalog listing shows the bundled Zen provider returning the curated static rows after live discovery timed out and fell back locally.

```text
$ node scripts/run-node.mjs models list --provider opencode
[fetch-timeout] fetch timeout after 5000ms (elapsed 5001ms) operation=fetchWithSsrFGuard url=https://opencode.ai/zen/v1/models
Model                                      Input      Ctx         Local Auth  Tags
opencode/claude-fable-5                    text+image 195k        no    yes
opencode/claude-opus-4-6                   text+image 195k        no    yes
opencode/claude-opus-4-7                   text+image 195k        no    yes
opencode/claude-opus-4-8                   text+image 195k        no    yes
opencode/claude-sonnet-4-6                 text+image 195k        no    yes

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  10 passed (10)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 56.66s
oxlint produced no output
plugins:inventory:check completed successfully
```

**Observed result after fix:** `openclaw models list --provider opencode` shows five `opencode/*` Zen model rows from the curated fallback catalog, and focused tests verify static seed resolution, cached live discovery, live-only row synthesis, shared OpenCode auth handling, runtime discovery metadata, and stale Zen base URL canonicalization.

**What was not tested:** Real OpenCode credentials, live `/zen/v1/models` success against the service, and live model completions were not run. Full `pnpm build` was attempted locally, but the `tsdown` phase was killed with SIGTERM after a long no-output period without a TypeScript diagnostic, so it is not claimed as passing here.
target.author: mushuiyu886
